subtitle: Books that have shaped how I think about physics, technology, philosophy, human behavior & psychology, and building.

books.title: The Principles of Quantum Mechanics
books.author: P.A.M. Dirac
books.note: I keep coming back to this. Nobody has stated the framework more cleanly since.

books.title: Optics
books.author: Eugene Hecht
books.note: My most-used reference. If I need to re-derive something in Fourier optics or interferometry, this is where I go.

books.title: Biomedical Optics
books.author: Lihong V. Wang & Hsin-i Wu
books.note: How I learned tissue optics and the photoacoustic effect. Covers light transport with enough rigor to actually build systems from.

books.title: The Hard Thing About Hard Things
books.author: Ben Horowitz
books.note: Read this during a rough stretch. Most startup books are about strategy; this one is about what happens when strategy fails.

books.title: Zero to One
books.author: Peter Thiel
books.note: Short and opinionated. The best part is the framing around secrets, what important truths do few people agree with you on.

books.title: The Innovator's Dilemma
books.author: Clayton Christensen
books.note: Required reading if you want to understand why good companies lose to worse products.

books.title: The Structure of Scientific Revolutions
books.author: Thomas S. Kuhn
books.note: Made me realize how much of what we call 'normal science' is puzzle-solving within assumptions nobody questions.

books.title: Gödel, Escher, Bach
books.author: Douglas Hofstadter
books.note: Changed how I think about self-reference and formal systems. Dense and worth rereading.

books.title: The Emperor's New Mind
books.author: Roger Penrose
books.note: Penrose arguing that consciousness is non-computable. I don't fully agree, but the argument is worth wrestling with.

books.title: Surely You're Joking, Mr. Feynman!
books.author: Richard Feynman
books.note: The reason I got into physics.

books.title: The Making of the Atomic Bomb
books.author: Richard Rhodes
books.note: The best science history book I've read. Shows how theoretical physics became the most consequential engineering project in history.

books.title: Elon Musk
books.author: Walter Isaacson
books.note: Complicated person, useful case study for thinking about deep-tech at scale.
